findings.line: He asked it like a commander because asking it any other way would have broken him.
Because living long enough to speak later, as Uther had told her in the corridor, was the only work left that might still matter.
There was no first glance that turned kindly.
findings.povMismatch: This zooms out into a general claim about how the tableau reads instead of staying with Jaina's immediate fear and visible evidence.

findings.line: He took in the king, and whatever in him had remained held together only because action was still possible broke in one silent visible place across his face.
findings.povMismatch: Jaina can read shock on Uther's face, but this polished sentence diagnoses his inner structure in a way that sounds authored from outside him.
findings.suggestedFix: He took in the king and went still.

findings.line: He asked it like a commander because asking it any other way would have broken him.
findings.povMismatch: The sentence explains Uther's psychological need in polished retrospective terms instead of staying with the command tone Jaina hears.

findings.line: Because living long enough to speak later, as Uther had told her in the corridor, was the only work left that might still matter.
findings.povMismatch: In this exhausted surrender beat, Jaina would think in blunt survival terms rather than this abstract summary about the only work left.

findings.line: Whatever was happening in the throne room had already started.
findings.missingPayload: The line never names the concrete event Jaina is inferring from the sharpened rhythm in the walls.
findings.reason: Placeholder-event summary with no local cash-out.

findings.line: look at the room, we killed him, you're standing in the answer.
findings.failedQuestion: What does this cash out to?
findings.missingPayload: The fragment never names the actual evidence profile in front of the guards; it leans on 'answer' as a stand-in.
findings.reason: Placeholder noun used where the thought needs the concrete proof in the room.
findings.suggestedFix: look at the room, we killed him.

audits.cleanReason: No translation-setup lines or plain-speech prompts appear in the chapter, so there is no exchange for the guide to audit.

summary.verdictReason: Pass B still has 8 mild KILL findings across GPT-5 prose issues, narrator seep, and phantom concreteness; more than 3 mild-only kills fails the pass.
